title: erodium cicutarium subsp. praetermissum
intro: Erodium cicutarium subsp. praetermissum, also known as the pink-stemmed storksbill or prickly geranium, is a plant species belonging to the geranium family (Geraniaceae) and is characterized by its pink flowers and sharp leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are believed to aid in wound healing and reducing swelling. The health benefits of Erodium cicutarium subsp. praetermissum include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
